title: Strolid Leadership Call
summary: The Q1 financial review revealed lower bookings and declining revenue, necessitating an organizational restructuring. New Performance Manager roles were established under Operational Directors. Strategic discussions included the Call Review acquisition, emphasizing a platform-centric narrative, and the demonstration of AI agent capabilities to improve workflow and data validation. Budgeting for AI usage and progress on the BDS deal and SOC compliance were also key topics.

decisions.description: Operational structure finalized: Performance Managers report to Operational Directors under Shawna Behen.

actionItems.task: Think through potential challenges with the new performance manager structure.
Reach out to Jamie to connect Joe Furnari regarding Call Review items.
Review Kevin Breen's Lovable report and authorize sharing with the team.
Spend more time with Sergey Lyapustin and Scott on AI tools guard rails.
Discuss and create a budget for extensive AI usage.
Share a concrete roadmap for SOC compliance after kickoff meeting.
Talk to Joe Furnari about setting a flexible and variable AI budget.
Respond to remaining items for the Better Dead Solutions (BDS) deal.

tensions: Q1 bookings and revenue are down year-over-year, requiring significant improvements.
Concern about Performance Managers having enough time for upselling with Jason Branham.
Vinnie Micciche expressed uneasiness about the performance manager role's execution.
Need for a clear budget for AI usage costs due to extensive internal use.
AI tool's power requires limiting access to prevent accidental issues.
Rosenberg's concern about generic email templates instead of personalized replies.
Business struggles affecting employee earnings and the need to tackle hard problems.
Critical need for the BDS deal; failure could require Perry to raise funds quickly.
BDC managers were previously overburdened with administrative work.
referencesToPast: Revenue has declined for the 4th consecutive month since October.
Churn last year in late Q1 and through Q2 had improved dramatically.
Q1 bookings are about half of the previous year's bookings.
Vinnie mentioned last week that revenue would continue to decline.
Operational structure was discussed last week.
BDC managers were previously overburdened with administrative work.
Sergey was helpful in the fall building a roadmap for administrative tasks.
Personalization has been on the roadmap for a while.
They had 20% bookings growth last year.
Joe Furnari and Vinnie Micciche discussed past churn reduction goals (50% to 35% to 25%).
Operational Directors Edwin, Gus, and Derek were BDC managers previously.
Seasonal trends for churn have been observed over the last couple of years.

contributions.description: Proposed offering full sales BDC for poor-performing stores to get a 'foot in the door'.
Presented Q1 financial recap, full-year goals, and targets for bookings, churn, and revenue.
Emphasized organizational adjustments were necessary due to BDC managers' administrative burden.
Highlighted the need for improved personalization in customer communication beyond first lead response.
Demonstrated how AI agents can shorten feedback loops for investigations and data validation.
Showcased an AI-generated report revealing 17.5% Sophia engagement with SMS replies.
Stressed keeping reports simple for dealers, focusing on unique customers for engagement rates.
Suggested positioning the company for a partnership if not in the next Call Review conversation.
Noted the need to budget for AI usage costs due to extensive internal use.
Confirmed the contract with SOC auditors is signed and a kickoff meeting is scheduled.
Provided an update on the Better Dead Solutions (BDS) deal, anticipating completion soon.
Requested a shortlist of Augusta merchandise items from Jason Branham.
